Class AppElse

Namespace
BOOSEapp
Assembly
BOOSEapp.dll

Represents the "else" branch of a conditional command within a program sequence.

public class AppElse : CompoundCommand, ICommand

Remarks

AppElse is used in conjunction with an "if" command to define alternative execution logic when the "if" condition is not met. It must directly follow a corresponding "if" command; otherwise, a BOOSE.CommandException is thrown during compilation.

This class is typically used internally by the command processing framework and is not intended to be instantiated directly by user code.

Constructors

AppElse()

Initialises a new instance of the AppElse class, and sets the conditional types to CommIF.

public AppElse()

Methods

Compile()

Compiles the "else" command by locating its matching "if" command on the program stack, linking them together, and registering the "else" command in the program sequence. If no matching "if" command is found, or if the found command is not an "if", a BOOSE.CommandException is thrown indicating a syntax error in the program.

public override void Compile()

Exceptions

CommandException

Execute()

Executes the "else" command by checking the condition of its corresponding "if" command. If the "if" condition is false, the program counter is set to the line number immediately following the "else" command, allowing execution to continue with the commands within the "else" block. If the "if" condition is true, execution continues sequentially, effectively skipping the "else" block.

public override void Execute()
